title: Encoder-Decoder Structure
summary: Full transformer: encoder (N blocks, bidirectional, no mask) produces E_enc. Decoder (N blocks, 3 sub-layers: masked self-attn + cross-attn + FFN) generates output autoregressively. Cross-attention bridges them. Encoder runs once, decoder runs T times at inference.
key_insights: Encoder: comprehend input fully (bidirectional). Decoder: generate output correctly (causal)
Decoder has 3 sub-layers per block vs encoder's 2 — extra is cross-attention
Same E_enc fed to ALL decoder blocks — each with its own W_K_cross, W_V_cross
Exposure bias: training sees correct tokens, inference sees own predictions
Decoder block ~33% more parameters than encoder block

mathematical_core.encoder_block: X_out = X_mid + FFN(LN(X_mid)),  where X_mid = X + MultiHead(LN(X))
mathematical_core.decoder_block: Y_out = Y_mid2 + FFN(LN(Y_mid2)),  where Y_mid2 = Y_mid1 + CrossAttn(LN(Y_mid1), E_enc),  Y_mid1 = Y + MaskedMultiHead(LN(Y))
mathematical_core.cross_attention_bridge: Q = LN(Y_mid1) · W_Q_cross,  K = E_enc · W_K_cross,  V = E_enc · W_V_cross
mathematical_core.output: P = softmax(LN(Y_final) · W_vocab),  P ∈ [B, n_tgt, V]

derivations.title: Parameter count comparison

steps.name: Encoder block
steps.math: Self-attn: 4d² (W_Q,K,V,O). FFN: 2·d·d_ff. LN×2: 4d. Total ≈ 12d² for d_ff=4d.

steps.name: Decoder block
steps.math: Self-attn: 4d². Cross-attn: 4d². FFN: 2·d·d_ff. LN×3: 6d. Total ≈ 16d² for d_ff=4d.

steps.name: Ratio
steps.math: Decoder/Encoder = 16d²/12d² ≈ 1.33. Decoder has 33% more params per block.

steps.name: Full model (N=6)
steps.math: Encoder: 6×12d² = 72d². Decoder: 6×16d² = 96d². Embeddings: V×d (often shared). Total ≈ 168d² + V·d.

derivations.consequence: For d=512: ~44M params (encoder) + ~50M params (decoder) + ~26M (embeddings, V=50k) ≈ 120M total. This is roughly the size of the original transformer.

derivations.title: Inference cost analysis — why autoregressive is expensive

steps.name: Encoder
steps.math: Runs once: O(N · n_src² · d) for self-attention + O(N · n_src · d · d_ff) for FFN.

steps.name: Decoder without KV-cache
steps.math: For each new token t: recompute self-attention over all t tokens. Total for T tokens: Σ_{t=1}^{T} O(t² · d) = O(T³ · d). Cubic in output length!

steps.name: Decoder with KV-cache
steps.math: Cache previous K,V for self-attention. Each new token: O(t · d) for self-attention (attend new token to cached keys), O(n_src · d) for cross-attention. Total: O(T² · d + T · n_src · d). Quadratic, not cubic.

derivations.consequence: KV-caching reduces decoder cost from O(T³) to O(T²). For long outputs, this is essential. Cache memory: O(N · T · d) for self-attention + O(N · n_src · d) for cross-attention keys/values.

geometric_properties: Encoder and decoder operate in same d_model space but develop different internal geometries
Cross-attention W_K, W_V projections bridge the two geometries
Monitor cross-attention entropy: high entropy = decoder can't find relevant encoder info (poor alignment)
statistical_properties: Monitor relative magnitudes of three sub-layer deltas: ||self_attn||, ||cross_attn||, ||FFN|| — if one dominates, others may be underutilized
Cross-attention weight stability during autoregressive generation — early tokens' patterns should remain stable
Attention to padding/EOS tokens increasing = model 'running out of information'
numerical_properties: E_enc memory: B × n_src × d × 4 bytes (stored for entire decoding process)
KV-cache memory grows linearly with generated length
For B=32, n_src=512, d=512: E_enc alone = 33.5MB
